sunnah_ref_en: Abu Waqid Al-Harith bin 'Auf (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) was sitting in the mosque with his Companions when three people came to him. Two of them stepped forward to the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) and the third went away. Those two men stood by the side of Messenger of Allah (ﷺ). One of them found a space in the circle and he filled it, while the other one sat behind him. When the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) finished, he said, "Shall I not inform you about these three people? One of them sought refuge with Allah and Allah gave him refuge; the second one felt shy and Allah showed kindness to his shyness (and so he was accommodated in that meeting), and the last one averted, and so Allah turned away His Attention from him.".
iwh_en: Abu Waqid Al-Harith bin 'Auf (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was sitting in the mosque with his Companions when three people came to him. Two of them stepped forward to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and the third went away. Those two men stood by the side of Messenger of Allah (ﷺ). One of them found a space in the circle and he filled it, while the other one sat behind him. When the Messenger of Allah ﷺ finished, he said, "Shall I not inform you about these three people? One of them sought refuge with Allah and Allah gave him refuge; the second one felt shy and Allah showed kindness to his shyness (and so he was accommodated in that meeting), and the last one averted, and so Allah turned away His Attention from him.".
iwh_id: Abu Waqid Al-Harith bin 'Auf (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ was sitting in the mosque with his Companions when three orang-orang came to him. Two of them stepped forward to Rasulullah ﷺ dan the third went away. Those two men berdiri by the side of Rasulullah ﷺ. One of them menemukan a space in the circle dan ia filled it, while the other one duduk di belakang him. Ketika Rasulullah ﷺ finished, he bersabda, "Shall I not inform you tentang these three orang-orang? One of them mencari refuge with Allah and Allah memberikan him refuge; the second one felt shy and Allah menunjukkan kindness to his rasa malu (and jadi he was accommodated in that meeting), and the terakhir one averted, and jadi Allah berpaling His Attention from him.".

chapter_en: The Book of the Remembrance of Allah
ar: وعن أبي سعيد الخدري رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ خرج معاوية رضي الله عنه على حلقة في المسجد، فقال‏:‏ ما أجلسكم‏؟‏ قالوا‏:‏ جلسنا نذكر الله‏.‏ قال‏:‏ آلله ما أجلسكم إلا ذاك‏؟‏ قالوا‏:‏ ما أجلسنا إلا ذاك، قال‏:‏ أما إني لم أستحلفكم تهمة لكم، وما كان أحد بمنزلتي من رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم أقل حديثًا مني‏:‏ إن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم خرج على حلقة من أصحابه فقال‏:‏ ‏"‏ما أجلسكم‏؟‏‏"‏ قالوا‏:‏ جلسنا نذكر الله، ونحمده على ما هدانا للإسلام، ومنَّ به علينا‏.‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏آلله ما أجلسكم إلا ذاك‏؟‏‏"‏ قالوا‏:‏ والله ما أجلسنا إلا ذلك‏"‏‏.‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏أما إني لم أستحلفكم تهمة لكم، ولكنه أتاني جبريل فأخبرني أن الله يباهي بكم الملائكة‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: Mu'awiyah (May Allah be pleased with him) came to an assembly in the mosque and asked them: "What has made you sit together?" They replied, "We are sitting here to remember Allah." He said, "I adjure you by Allah to tell me whether nothing else has made you to sit together." They replied: "By Allah! We are sitting only to remember Allah." Then he said, "I did not adjure you because I suspected you. No one of my rank in the eyes of the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) is the narrator of so few traditions as I am. The fact is that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) went out to a circle of his Companions and said, 'What has made you sit there?' When they replied that they are sitting together to remember Allah and to praise Him for guiding them to Islam and bestowing favours on them, he (ﷺ) said, 'I adjure you by Allah to tell me that nothing else has made you sit together.' On their reply that there was certainly no other purpose, he (ﷺ) said, 'I did not adjure you because I suspected you, but Jibril (Gabriel) came to me and told me that Allah was talking proudly of you to the angels."'.
iwh_en: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that mu'awiyah (may Allah be pleased with him) came to an assembly in the mosque and asked them: "What has made you sit together?" They replied, "We are sitting here to remember Allah." He said, "I adjure you by Allah to tell me whether nothing else has made you to sit together." They replied: "By Allah! We are sitting only to remember Allah." Then he said, "I did not adjure you because I suspected you. No one of my rank in the eyes of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ is the narrator of so few traditions as I am. The fact is that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ went out to a circle of his Companions and said, 'What has made you sit there?' When they replied that they are sitting together to remember Allah and to praise Him for guiding them to Islam and bestowing favours on them, he (ﷺ) said, 'I adjure you by Allah to tell me that nothing else has made you sit together.' On their reply that there was certainly no other purpose, he (ﷺ) said, 'I did not adjure you because I suspected you, but Jibril (Gabriel) came to me and told me that Allah was talking proudly of you to the angels."'.
iwh_id: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa mu'awiyah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) datang kepada an assembly di masjid dan bertanya them: "What has membuat you duduk together?" They menjawab, "We are sitting here to remember Allah." He berkata, "I adjure you by Allah to tell me whether tidak ada else has membuat you to duduk together." They menjawab: "By Allah! We are sitting only to remember Allah." Kemudian he berkata, "I tidak adjure you because I suspected you. No one of my rank di eyes of Rasulullah ﷺ is the narrator of jadi few traditions as I am. The fact is that Rasulullah ﷺ pergi out kepada circle of para sahabatnya dan berkata, 'What has membuat you duduk there?' Ketika mereka menjawab that they are sitting together to remember Allah dan to memuji-Nya for guiding them to Islam dan bestowing favours on them, he (ﷺ) berkata, 'I adjure you by Allah to tell me that tidak ada else has membuat you duduk together.' On their reply that there was certainly no other purpose, he (ﷺ) berkata, 'I tidak adjure you because I suspected you, namun Jibril (Gabriel) datang kepada me dan memberitahuku that Allah was talking proudly of you kepada angels."'.

chapter_en: The Book of the Remembrance of Allah
ar:
وعن أبي هريرة رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم‏:‏ ‏
"‏من قال حين يصبح وحين يمسي‏:‏ سبحان الله وبحمده مائة مرة، لم يأتِ أحد يوم القيامة بأفضل مما جاء به، إلا أحد قال مثل ما قال أو زاد‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏ رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

isnad_chain.name_en: Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him]

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "He who recites in the morning and in the evening the statement: 'Subhan-Allahi wa bihamdihi (Allah is free from imperfection and I begin with praising Him)' one hundred times, will not be surpassed on the Day of Resurrection by anyone with better deeds than one who utters the same words or utters more of these words.".
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "He who recites in the morning and in the evening the statement: 'Subhan-Allahi wa bihamdihi (Allah is free from imperfection and I begin with praising Him)' one hundred times, will not be surpassed on the Day of Resurrection by anyone with better deeds than one who utters the same words or utters more of these words.".
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "He who recites in the morning dan in the evening the statement: 'Subhan-Allahi wa bihamdihi (Allah is free from imperfection dan aku memulai dengan praising Him)' one hundred times, tidak akan be surpassed di Hari Kebangkitan by anyone with better deeds than one who utters the same words or utters more of these words.".

chapter_en: The Book of the Remembrance of Allah
ar:
وعنه قال‏:‏ جاء رجل إلى النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم، فقال‏:‏ يا رسول الله ما لقيت من عقرب لدغتني البارحة‏!‏ قال‏:‏ ‏
"‏أما لو قلت حين أمسيت‏:‏ أعوذ بكلمات الله التامات من شر ما خلق لم تضرك‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

وعنه قال‏:‏ جاء رجل إلى النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم، فقال‏:‏ يا رسول الله ما لقيت من عقرب لدغتني البارحة‏!‏ قال‏:‏ ‏
"‏أما لو قلت حين أمسيت‏:‏ أعوذ بكلمات الله التامات من شر ما خلق لم تضرك‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: A man came to the Prophet (ﷺ) and said, "What a trouble I suffered from a scorpion which stung me last night!" He (ﷺ) said, "Had you said in the evening: 'A'udhu bikalimatillahit-tammati min sharri ma khalaqa (I seek the protection of Allah's Perfect Words from the evil of whatever He has created),' it would not have harmed you.".
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that a man came to the Prophet ﷺ and said, "What a trouble I suffered from a scorpion which stung me last night!" He (ﷺ) said, "Had you said in the evening: 'A'udhu bikalimatillahit-tammati min sharri ma khalaqa (I seek the protection of Allah's Perfect Words from the evil of whatever He has created),' it would not have harmed you.".
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melaporkan bahwa seorang laki-laki datang kepada Nabi ﷺ dan berkata, "What a trouble I suffered from a scorpion which stung me terakhir night!" He (ﷺ) berkata, "Had you berkata in the evening: 'A'udhu bikalimatillahit-tammati min sharri ma khalaqa (I mencari the protection of Allah's Perfect Words from the evil of whatever He has created),' it tidak akan have harmed you.".

chapter_en: The Book of the Remembrance of Allah
ar: وعنه عنه النبي، صلى الله عليه وسلم، أنه كان يقول إذا أصبح‏:‏ ‏"‏اللهم بك أصبحنا، وبك أمسينا، وبك نحيا، وبك نموت، وإليك النشور‏"‏‏.‏ وإذا أمسى قال‏:‏ ‏"‏اللهم بك أمسينا، وبك نحيا، وبك نموت، وإليك المصير‏"‏‏.‏ رواه أبو داود والترمذي وقال حديث حسن‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Prophet (ﷺ) used to say in the morning: "Allahumma bika asbahna, wa bika amsaina, wa bika nahya, wa bika namutu, wa ilaikan-nushur (O Allah! With Your Power we have come to the morning, with Your Power we come to the evening, with Your Power we live, and we die, and to You will we return)." In the evening he would say: "Allahumma bika amsaina, wa bika nahya, wa bika namutu, wa ilaikan-nushur (O Allah! With Your Power, we have come to the evening, by You do we live, by You do we die, and to You is the return).".

With Your Power we have come to the morning, with Your Power we come to the evening, with Your Power we live, and we die, and to You will we return)." In the evening he would say: "Allahumma bika amsaina, wa bika nahya, wa bika namutu, wa ilaikan-nushur (O Allah! With Your Power, we have come to the evening, by You do we live, by You do we die, and to You is the return).".
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ biasa berkata di morning: "Allahumma bika asbahna, wa bika amsaina, wa bika nahya, wa bika namutu, wa ilaikan-nushur (Ya Allah! With Your Power we have come kepada morning, with Your Power we come kepada evening, with Your Power we live, dan kami die, and to You will we return)." In the evening ia berkata: "Allahumma bika amsaina, wa bika nahya, wa bika namutu, wa ilaikan-nushur (Ya Allah! With Your Power, we have come kepada evening, by You do we live, by You do we die, and to You is the return).".

chapter_en: The Book of the Remembrance of Allah
ar: وعنه أن أبا بكر الصديق رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ يا رسول الله مرني بكلمات أقولهن إذا أصبحت وإذا أمسيت، قال‏:‏ قل‏:‏ ‏"‏اللهم فاطر السماوات والأرض عالم الغيب والشهادة، رب كل شيء ومليكه أشهد أن لا إله إلا أنت، أعوذ بك من شر نفسي وشر الشيطان وشركه‏"‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏قلها إذا أصبحت، وإذا أمسيت، وإذا أخذت مضجعك‏"‏‏.‏ رواه أبو داود والترمذي وقال حديث حسن صحيح‏.‏
ar_clean: وعنه أن أبا بكر الصديق رضي الله عنه قال: يا رسول الله مرني بكلمات أقولهن إذا أصبحت وإذا أمسيت، قال: قل: "اللهم فاطر السماوات والأرض عالم الغيب والشهادة، رب كل شيء ومليكه أشهد أن لا إله إلا أنت، أعوذ بك من شر نفسي وشر الشيطان وشركه" قال: "قلها إذا أصبحت، وإذا أمسيت، وإذا أخذت مضجعك". رواه أبو داود والترمذي وقال حديث حسن صحيح.

matn_ar_clean: وعنه أن أبا بكر الصديق رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ يا رسول الله مرني بكلمات أقولهن إذا أصبحت وإذا أمسيت، قال‏:‏ قل‏:‏ ‏"‏اللهم فاطر السماوات والأرض عالم الغيب والشهادة، رب كل شيء ومليكه أشهد أن لا إله إلا أنت، أعوذ بك من شر نفسي وشر الشيطان وشركه‏"‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏قلها إذا أصبحت، وإذا أمسيت، وإذا أخذت مضجعك‏"‏‏.‏ رواه أبو داود والترمذي وقال حديث حسن صحيح‏.‏
sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: Abu Bakr asked, "O Messenger of Allah! Teach me some words so that I may recite them in the morning and in the evening." The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Recite these words: 'Allahumma fatiras-samawati wal-ardi, 'alimal-ghaibi wash-shahadati, Rabba kulli shai'in wa malikahu. Ash- hadu an la ilaha illa Anta, a'udhu bika min sharri nafsi, wa sharrish- Shaitani wa shirkihi (O Allah! Creator of the heavens and the earth! Knower of the hidden and the exposed! Rubb of everything and every one. I bear witness that none has the right to be worshipped but You. I seek Your Protection from the evil of my own self from the evil of Satan and from the evil of Shirk to which he calls)."' The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) added: "Recite these words in the morning and the evening and when you go to bed.".
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that abu Bakr asked, "O Messenger of Allah! Teach me some words so that I may recite them in the morning and in the evening." the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Recite these words: 'Allahumma fatiras-samawati wal-ardi, 'alimal-ghaibi wash-shahadati, Rabba kulli shai'in wa malikahu. Ash- hadu an la ilaha illa Anta, a'udhu bika min sharri nafsi, wa sharrish- Shaitani wa shirkihi (O Allah! Creator of the heavens and the earth! Knower of the hidden and the exposed! Rubb of everything and every one. I bear witness that none has the right to be worshipped but You. I seek Your Protection from the evil of my own self from the evil of Satan and from the evil of Shirk to which he calls)."' the Messenger of Allah ﷺ added: "Recite these words in the morning and the evening and when you go to bed.".
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa abu Bakr bertanya, "O Rasulullah! Teach me some words sehingga I may membaca them in the morning dan in the evening." Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "Recite these words: 'Allahumma fatiras-samawati wal-ardi, 'alimal-ghaibi mencuci-shahadati, Rabba kulli shai'in wa malikahu. Ash- hadu an la ilaha illa Anta, a'udhu bika min sharri nafsi, wa sharrish- Shaitani wa shirkihi (Ya Allah! Creator of the heavens dan the earth! Knower of the hidden dan the exposed! Rubb of everything dan every one. I bear witness that none has the kanan to be worshipped namun You. I mencari Your Protection from the evil of my own self from the evil of Satan dan from the evil of Shirk to which he calls)."' Rasulullah ﷺ added: "Recite these words in the morning dan the evening dan when you go to bed.".

chapter_en: The Book of the Remembrance of Allah
ar: وعن ابن مسعود رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ كان نبي الله صلى الله عليه وسلم، إذا أمسى قال‏:‏ أمسينا وأمسى الملك لله، والحمد لله، لا إله إلا الله وحده لا شريك له‏"‏ قال الراوي‏:‏ أراه قال فيهن‏:‏ ‏"‏له الملك وله الحمد وهو على كل شيء قدير، رب أسألك خير ما في هذه الليلة، وخير ما بعدها، وأعوذ بك من شر ما في هذه الليلة وشر ما بعدها، رب أعوذ بك من الكسل، وسوء الكبر، رب أعوذ بك من شر ما في هذه الليلة، وخير ما بعدها، وأعوذ بك من شر ما في هذه الليلة وشر ما بعدها، رب أعوذ بك من الكسل، وسوء الكبر، رب أعوذ بك من عذاب النار، وعذاب في القبر‏"‏ وإذا أصبح قال ذلك أيضًا‏:‏ ‏"‏أصبحنا وأصبح الملك لله‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

isnad_ar: وعن ابن مسعود رضي الله عنه قال: كان نبي الله صلى الله عليه وسلم

sunnah_ref_en: 'Abdullah bin Mas'ud (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: When it was evening, the Prophet (ﷺ) used to supplicate: "Amsaina wa amsal-mulku lillah, wal-hamdu lillah. La 'ilaha illallahu wahdahu la sharika lahu (We have entered upon evening and the whole kingdom of Allah, too, has entered upon evening. Praise is due to Allah. There is none who has the right to be worshiped but Allah, the One who has no partner with Him)." He (the narrator) said: I think that he (ﷺ) used to follow the recitation with these words: "Lahul-mulku, wa lahul-hamdu, wa Huwa 'ala kulli shi'in Qadir. Rabbi as'aluka khaira ma fi hadhihil-lailati, wa khaira ma ba'daha; wa a'udhu bika min sharri ma fi hadhihil-lailati, wa sharri ma ba'daha; Rabbi a'udhu bika minal- kasali, wa su'il-kibari; Rabbi a'udhu bika min 'adhabin fin-nari, wa 'adhabin fil-qabri (His is the sovereignty and to Him is all praise due, and He is Omnipotent. My Rubb, I beg of you good that lies in this night and good that follows it, and I seek refuge in You from the evil that lies in this night and from the evil of that which follows it. My Rubb! I seek refuge in You from lethargy and the misery of old age. O Allah! I seek Your Protection from the torment of Hell-fire and the punishment of the grave)." When it was morning, he (ﷺ) would recite the same, replacing the words: "We have entered upon evening and the whole kingdom of Allah, too, has entered upon evening" with "We have entered upon morning and the whole kingdom of Allah entered upon morning." (Replace the words "Amsaina, amsa, hadhihil-lailati, ma ba'daha with Asbahna, asbaha, hadhal-yaumi, ma ba'dahu, respectively.).

La 'ilaha illallahu wahdahu la sharika lahu (We have entered upon evening and the whole kingdom of Allah, too, has entered upon evening. Praise is due to Allah. There is none who has the right to be worshiped but Allah, the One who has no partner with Him)." He (the narrator) said: I think that he (ﷺ) used to follow the recitation with these words: "Lahul-mulku, wa lahul-hamdu, wa Huwa 'ala kulli shi'in Qadir. Rabbi as'aluka khaira ma fi hadhihil-lailati, wa khaira ma ba'daha; wa a'udhu bika min sharri ma fi hadhihil-lailati, wa sharri ma ba'daha; Rabbi a'udhu bika minal- kasali, wa su'il-kibari; Rabbi a'udhu bika min 'adhabin fin-nari, wa 'adhabin fil-qabri (His is the sovereignty and to Him is all praise due, and He is Omnipotent. My Rubb, I beg of you good that lies in this night and good that follows it, and I seek refuge in You from the evil that lies in this night and from the evil of that which follows it. My Rubb! I seek refuge in You from lethargy and the misery of old age. O Allah! I seek Your Protection from the torment of Hell-fire and the punishment of the grave)." When it was morning, he (ﷺ) would recite the same, replacing the words: "We have entered upon evening and the whole kingdom of Allah, too, has entered upon evening" with "We have entered upon morning and the whole kingdom of Allah entered upon morning." (Replace the words "Amsaina, amsa, hadhihil-lailati, ma ba'daha with Asbahna, asbaha, hadhal-yaumi, ma ba'dahu, respectively.).
iwh_id:
'Abdullah bin Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan:
When it was evening, Nabi ﷺ biasa supplicate: "Amsaina wa amsal-mulku lillah, wal-hamdu lillah. La 'ilaha illallahu wahdahu la sharika lahu (We have entered upon evening dan the whole kingdom of Allah, too, has entered upon evening. Praise is due to Allah. There is none who has the kanan to be worshiped namun Allah, the One who has no partner with Him)." He (the narrator) berkata: I berpikir that he (ﷺ) biasa follow the recitation with these words: "Lahul-mulku, wa lahul-hamdu, wa Huwa 'ala kulli shi'in Qadir. Rabbi as'aluka khaira ma fi hadhihil-lailati, wa khaira ma ba'daha; wa a'udhu bika min sharri ma fi hadhihil-lailati, wa sharri ma ba'daha; Rabbi a'udhu bika minal- kasali, wa su'il-kibari; Rabbi a'udhu bika min 'adhabin fin-nari, wa 'adhabin fil-qabri (His is the sovereignty dan to Him is all praise due, and He is Omnipotent. My Rubb, I beg of you baik that lies in this night dan baik that follows it, and Saya berlindung in You from the evil that lies in this night dan from the evil of that which follows it. My Rubb! Saya berlindung in You from lethargy dan the misery of tua age. Ya Allah! I mencari Your Protection from the torment of Neraka dan the punishment of the grave)." When it was morning, he (ﷺ) would membaca the same, replacing the words: "We have entered upon evening dan the whole kingdom of Allah, too, has entered upon evening" with "We have entered upon morning dan the whole kingdom of Allah entered upon morning." (Replace the words "Amsaina, amsa, hadhihil-lailati, ma ba'daha with Asbahna, asbaha, hadhal-yaumi, ma ba'dahu, respectively.).

chapter_en: The Book of the Remembrance of Allah
ar:
وعن عبد الله بن خُبيب -بضم الخاء المعجمة- رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ قال لي رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم‏:‏ ‏
"‏اقرأ‏:‏ قل هو الله أحد، والمعوذتين حين تمسي وحين تصبح، ثلاث مرات، تكفيك من كل شيء‏"‏‏.‏ رواه أبو داود والترمذي وقال حديث حسن صحيح ‏.‏

chapter_en: The Book of the Remembrance of Allah
ar:
وعن عثمان بن عفان رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم‏:‏ ‏
"‏ما من عبد يقول في صباح كل يوم ومساء كل ليلة‏:‏ بسم الله الذي لا يضر مع اسمه شيء في الأرض ولا في السماء وهو السميع العليم، ثلاث مرات، إلا لم يضره شيء‏"‏‏.‏ رواه أبو داود والترمذي وقال حديث حسن صحيح ‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: 'Uthman bin 'Affan (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "He who recites three times every morning and evening: 'Bismillahil-ladhi la yadurru ma'as-mihi shai'un fil-ardi wa la fis-sama'i, wa Huwas-Sami'ul-'Alim (In the Name of Allah with Whose Name there is protection against every kind of harm in the earth or in the heaven, and He is the All-Hearing and All- Knowing),' nothing will harm him.".

iwh_id: 'Uthman bin 'Affan (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan: Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "He who recites three times every morning dan evening: 'Bismillahil-ladhi la yadurru ma'as-mihi shai'un fil-ardi wa la fis-sama'i, wa Huwas-Sami'ul-'Alim (In the Name of Allah with Whose Name there is protection terhadap every kind of harm in the earth or in the heaven, and He is the All-Hearing and All- Knowing),' nothing will harm him.".

chapter_en: The Book of the Remembrance of Allah
ar:
وعن حذيفة وأبي ذر رضي الله عنهما أن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم كان إذا أوى إلى فراشه قال‏:‏ ‏
"‏باسمك اللهم أحيا وأموت‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه البخاري‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
ar_clean:
وعن حذيفة وأبي ذر رضي الله عنهما أن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم كان إذا أوى إلى فراشه قال: 
"باسمك اللهم أحيا وأموت" ((رواه البخاري)).
isnad_ar: وعن حذيفة وأبي ذر رضي الله عنهما أن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم كان إذا أوى إلى فراشه قال:
matn_ar: "باسمك اللهم أحيا وأموت" ((رواه البخاري)).
matn_ar_clean: "باسمك اللهم أحيا وأموت" ((رواه البخاري)).
sunnah_ref_en: Hudhaifah and Abu Dharr (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) used to supplicate when he went to bed at night: "Bismik-Allahumma ahya wa amut (With Your Name, O Allah, I expire and return to life).".
iwh_en: Hudhaifah and Abu Dharr (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to supplicate when he went to bed at night: "Bismik-Allahumma ahya wa amut (With Your Name, O Allah, I expire and return to life).".
iwh_id: Hudhaifah and Abu Dharr (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ biasa supplicate ketika ia pergi kepada bed at night: "Bismik-Allahumma ahya wa amut (With Your Name, Ya Allah, I expire dan return to life).".

chapter_en: The Book of the Remembrance of Allah
ar: وعن علي رضي الله عنه أن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم قال له ولفاطمة، رضي الله عنهما‏:‏ ‏"‏إذا أويتما إلى فراشكما، أو‏:‏ إذا أخذتما مضجعكما - فكبرا ثلاثًا وثلاثين، وسبحا ثلاثًا وثلاثين، واحمدا ثلاثًا وثلاثين‏"‏ وفي رواية‏:‏ التسبيح أربعًا وثلاثين‏"‏ وفي رواية‏:‏ ‏"‏التكبير أربعًا وثلاثين‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
ar_clean: وعن علي رضي الله عنه أن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم قال له ولفاطمة، رضي الله عنهما: "إذا أويتما إلى فراشكما، أو: إذا أخذتما مضجعكما - فكبرا ثلاثًا وثلاثين، وسبحا ثلاثًا وثلاثين، واحمدا ثلاثًا وثلاثين" وفي رواية: التسبيح أربعًا وثلاثين" وفي رواية: "التكبير أربعًا وثلاثين" ((متفق عليه)).
isnad_ar: وعن علي رضي الله عنه أن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم قال له ولفاطمة، رضي الله عنه
matn_ar: ما: "إذا أويتما إلى فراشكما، أو: إذا أخذتما مضجعكما - فكبرا ثلاثًا وثلاثين، وسبحا ثلاثًا وثلاثين، واحمدا ثلاثًا وثلاثين" وفي رواية: التسبيح أربعًا وثلاثين" وفي رواية: "التكبير أربعًا وثلاثين" ((متفق عليه)).
matn_ar_clean: ما: "إذا أويتما إلى فراشكما، أو: إذا أخذتما مضجعكما - فكبرا ثلاثا وثلاثين، وسبحا ثلاثا وثلاثين، واحمدا ثلاثا وثلاثين" وفي رواية: التسبيح أربعا وثلاثين" وفي رواية: "التكبير أربعا وثلاثين" ((متفق عليه)).
sunnah_ref_en: 'Ali (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said to me and to Fatimah (May Allah be pleased with her) (Ali's wife and the Prophet's daughter), "When you go to bed, recite: Takbir (Allahu Akbar) thirty-three times and Tasbih (Subhan-Allah) thirty-three times and Tahmid (Al-hamdu lillah) thirty-three times."Another narration is: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Recite Tasbih thirty-four times."Another narration is: The Messenger of Allah(ﷺ) said, "Recite Takbir thirty-four times.".
iwh_en: 'Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) reported: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to me and to Fatimah (may Allah be pleased with her) (Ali's wife and the Prophet ﷺ's daughter), "When you go to bed, recite: Takbir (Allahu Akbar) thirty-three times and Tasbih (Subhan-Allah) thirty-three times and Tahmid (Al-hamdu lillah) thirty-three times."Another narration is: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Recite Tasbih thirty-four times."Another narration is: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Recite Takbir thirty-four times.".
iwh_id:
'Ali (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan:
Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda kepadaku, and to Fatimah (radhiyallahu 'anha) (Ali's wife and Nabi ﷺ's putri), "When you go to bed, membaca: Takbir (Allahu Akbar) thirty-tiga kali and Tasbih (Subhan-Allah) thirty-tiga kali and Tahmid (Al-hamdu lillah) thirty-tiga kali."Another narration is: Rasulullah ﷺ berkata, "Recite Tasbih thirty-four times."Another narration is: Rasulullah ﷺ berkata, "Recite Takbir thirty-four times.".

chapter_en: The Book of the Remembrance of Allah
ar:
وعن أبي هريرة رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم‏:‏ ‏
"‏إذا أوى أحدكم إلى فراشه، فلينفض فراشه بداخلة إزاره فإنه لا يدري ما خلفه عليه، ثم يقول‏:‏ باسمك ربي وضعت جنبي، وبك أرفعه؛ إن أمسكت نفسي فارحمها، وإن أرسلتها، فاحفظها بما تحفظ به عبادك الصالحين‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

isnad_chain.name_en: Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him]

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "When any of you goes to bed, he should shake off (or dust off) his bedsheet because he does not know what might have fallen on it after he had left it. Then he should recite: 'Bismika Rabbi wada'tu janbi, wa bika arfa'uhu, in amsakta nafsi farhamha, wa in arsaltaha fahfazha bima tahfazu bihi 'ibadakas-salihin .
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "When any of you goes to bed, he should shake off (or dust off) his bedsheet because he does not know what might have fallen on it after he had left it. Then he should recite: 'Bismika Rabbi wada'tu janbi, wa bika arfa'uhu, in amsakta nafsi farhamha, wa in arsaltaha fahfazha bima tahfazu bihi 'ibadakas-salihin .
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "When any of you goes to bed, he should shake off (or dust off) his bedsheet because he does not mengetahui what might have fallen on it setelah he had kiri it. Kemudian he should membaca: 'Bismika Rabbi wada'tu janbi, wa bika arfa'uhu, in amsakta nafsi farhamha, wa in arsaltaha fahfazha bima tahfazu bihi 'ibadakas-salihin .

chapter_en: The Book of the Remembrance of Allah
ar:
وعن عائشة، رضي الله عنه، أن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم، كان إذا أخذ مضجعه نفث في يديه، وقرأ بالمعوذات ومسح بهما جسده‏.‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏ 
وفي رواية لهما‏:‏ ‏
"‏أن النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم، كان إذا أوى إلى فراشه كل ليلة جمع كفيه، ثم نفث فيهما فقرأ فيهما‏:‏ قل هو الله أحد، وقل أعوذ برب الفلق، وقل أعوذ برب الناس، ثم مسح بهما ما استطاع من جسده، يبدأ بهما على رأسه ووجهه، وما أقبل من جسده، يفعل ذلك ثلاث مرات‏"‏‏.‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏‏. قال أهل اللغة : (( النفث )) نفخ لطيف بلا ريق‏

sunnah_ref_en: 'A ishah (May Allah be pleased with her) reported: Whenever the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) went to bed, he would blow upon his hands recite Al-Mu'awwidhat; and pass his hands over his body..
iwh_en: 'A ishah (may Allah be pleased with her) reported: Whenever the Messenger of Allah ﷺ went to bed, he would blow upon his hands recite Al-Mu'awwidhat; and pass his hands over his body..
iwh_id:
'A ishah (radhiyallahu 'anha) meriwayatkan:
Setiap kali Rasulullah ﷺ pergi kepada bed, ia blow upon his hands membaca Al-Mu'awwidhat; dan pass his hands over his body..

chapter_en: The Book of the Remembrance of Allah
ar:
وعن البراء بن عازب رضي الله عنهما، قال‏:‏ قال لي رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم‏:‏ ‏
"‏إذا أتيت مضجعك فتوضأ وضوءك للصلاة، ثم اضطجع على شقك الأيمن، وقل‏:‏ اللهم أسلمت نفسي إليك، ووجهت وجهي إليك وفوضت أمري إليك، وألجأت ظهري إليك رغبة ورهبة إليك لا ملجأ ولا منجا منك إلا إليك آمنت بكتابك الذي أنزلت، وبنبيك الذي أرسلت، فإن مت، مت على الفطرة، واجعلهن آخر ما تقول‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

"إذا أتيت مضجعك فتوضأ وضوءك للصلاة، ثم اضطجع على شقك الأيمن، وقل: اللهم أسلمت نفسي إليك، ووجهت وجهي إليك وفوضت أمري إليك، وألجأت ظهري إليك رغبة ورهبة إليك لا ملجأ ولا منجا منك إلا إليك آمنت بكتابك الذي أنزلت، وبنبيك الذي أرسلت، فإن مت، مت على الفطرة، واجعلهن آخر ما تقول" ((متفق عليه)).
sunnah_ref_en: Al-Bara' bin 'Azib (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Whenever you intend to go to bed, perform Wudu' as is done for Salat (prayer); and then lie down on the right side and recite: 'Allahumma aslamtu nafsi ilaika, wa fawwadtu amri ilaika, wal-ja'tu zahri ilaika, raghbatan wa rahbatan ilaika, la malja'a wa la manja minka illa ilaika, amantu bikitabik-alladhi anzalta, wa binabiyyik-alladhi arsalta .
iwh_en: Al-Bara' bin 'Azib (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Whenever you intend to go to bed, perform Wudu' as is done for Salat (prayer); and then lie down on the right side and recite: 'Allahumma aslamtu nafsi ilaika, wa fawwadtu amri ilaika, wal-ja'tu zahri ilaika, raghbatan wa rahbatan ilaika, la malja'a wa la manja minka illa ilaika, amantu bikitabik-alladhi anzalta, wa binabiyyik-alladhi arsalta .
iwh_id: Al-Bara' bin 'Azib (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "Whenever you intend to go to bed, perform Wudu' as is done for Salat (prayer); dan kemudian lie down on the kanan side dan membaca: 'Allahumma aslamtu nafsi ilaika, wa fawwadtu amri ilaika, wal-ja'tu zahri ilaika, raghbatan wa rahbatan ilaika, la malja'a wa la manja minka illa ilaika, amantu bikitabik-alladhi anzalta, wa binabiyyik-alladhi arsalta .

chapter_en: The Book of the Remembrance of Allah
ar:
وعن أنس رضي الله عنه، أن النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم، كان إذا أوى فراشه قال‏:‏ ‏
"‏الحمد لله الذي أطعمنا وسقانا، وكفانا وآوانا، فكم ممن لا كافي له ولا مئوي‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

"الحمد لله الذي أطعمنا وسقانا، وكفانا وآوانا، فكم ممن لا كافي له ولا مئوي" ((رواه مسلم)).
matn_ar_clean:

"الحمد لله الذي أطعمنا وسقانا، وكفانا وآوانا، فكم ممن لا كافي له ولا مئوي" ((رواه مسلم)).
sunnah_ref_en: Anas (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: Whenever the Prophet (ﷺ) went to his bed, he would say: "Al-hamdu lillah-illadhi at'amana wa saqana, wa kafana wa awana, fakam mimman la kafiya lahu wa la mu'wiya (Praise is due to Allah Who has fed us, provided us drink, satisfied us and gave us protection. Many are those who have no one to provide for them, or give them shelter).".
iwh_en: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that whenever the Prophet ﷺ went to his bed, he would say: "Al-hamdu lillah-illadhi at'amana wa saqana, wa kafana wa awana, fakam mimman la kafiya lahu wa la mu'wiya (Praise is due to Allah Who has fed us, provided us drink, satisfied us and gave us protection. Many are those who have no one to provide for them, or give them shelter).".
iwh_id: Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa setiap kali Nabi ﷺ pergi kepada his bed, ia berkata: "Al-hamdu lillah-illadhi at'amana wa saqana, wa kafana wa awana, fakam mimman la kafiya lahu wa la mu'wiya (Praise is due to Allah Who has fed us, provided us drink, satisfied us dan memberikan us protection. Many are those yang have no one to provide for them, or memberikan them shelter).".

chapter_en: The Book of the Remembrance of Allah
ar:
وعن حذيفة رضي الله عنه أن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم، كان إذا أراد أن يرقد وضع يده اليمنى تحت خده، ثم يقول‏:‏ ‏
"‏اللهم قني عذابك يوم تبعث عبادك‏"‏‏.‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه الترمذي وقال حديث حسن ورواه أبو داود من رواية حفصة رضي الله عنه وفيه أنه كان يقوله ثلاث مرات‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Hudhaifah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: Whenever the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) intended to go to sleep, he would place his right hand under his (right) cheek and supplicate: "Allahumma qini 'adhabaka yauma tab'athu 'ibadaka (O Allah! Guard me against Your punishment on the Day when You will resurrect Your slaves).".In a narration in Abu Dawud, Hafsah (May Allah be pleased with her) said: Before going to sleep the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) would recite this Du'a three times.
iwh_en: Hudhaifah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that whenever the Messenger of Allah ﷺ intended to go to sleep, he would place his right hand under his (right) cheek and supplicate: "Allahumma qini 'adhabaka yauma tab'athu 'ibadaka (O Allah! Guard me against Your punishment on the Day when You will resurrect Your slaves).".In a narration in Abu Dawud, Hafsah (may Allah be pleased with her) said: Before going to sleep the Messenger of Allah ﷺ would recite this Du'a three times.
iwh_id: Hudhaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa setiap kali Rasulullah ﷺ intended to go to sleep, ia place his kanan hand under his (kanan) cheek dan supplicate: "Allahumma qini 'adhabaka yauma tab'athu 'ibadaka (Ya Allah! Guard me terhadap Your punishment di Day when You will resurrect Your slaves).".In a narration in Abu Dawud, Hafsah (radhiyallahu 'anha) berkata: Before going to sleep Rasulullah ﷺ would membaca this Du'a three times.
